title: salix rupifraga f. eriocarpa
intro: Salix rupifraga f. eriocarpa, also known as the fragrant willow or water willow, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it an effective natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory agent. The health benefits of Salix rupifraga f. eriocarpa include its ability to reduce fever, relieve headaches and menstrual cramps, and soothe digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and its extracts can also be used in topical applications to alleviate pain and inflammation.
